intended_use: JBS-LVO is a notification-only, parallel workflow tool for use by hospital networks and trained clinicians to identify and communicate images of specific patients to a specialist, independent of stand
intended_use_full: JBS-LVO is a notification-only, parallel workflow tool for use by hospital networks and trained clinicians to identify and communicate images of specific patients to a specialist, independent of standard of care workflow. JBS-LVO uses an artificial intelligence algorithm to analyze images for findings suggestive of a pre-specified clinical condition and to notify an appropriate medical specialist of these findings in parallel to standard of care image interpretation. Specifically, the device analyzes CT angiogram images of the brain acquired in the acute setting and sends notifications to a neurovascular specialist that a suspected large vessel occlusion has been identified and recommends a review of those images.
target_condition: Large vessel occlusion (LVO) of the intracranial carotid artery (ICA) and middle cerebral artery (MCA-M1 segment)
ground_truth: Two US board-certified neuroradiologists with a third intervening in cases of disagreement
other_metrics: Mean CTA-to-notification time: 2.95 minutes (95% CI: 2.89-3.02); 95% CI for sensitivity: 85.8% to 95.8%; 95% CI for specificity: 87.2% to 96.5%; 95% CI for AUC: 93.0% to 98.1%
